steps.title: Find the circle's area
steps.content:
Use the circle area formula $A = \pi r^2$ with radius $r = 2$:
$$A_{\text{circle}} = \pi(2)^2 = 4\pi$$

steps.title: Set up and solve for the square's side
steps.content:
The square has the same area as the circle, so if $s$ is the side length:
$$s^2 = 4\pi$$
Taking the square root of both sides:
$$s = \sqrt{4\pi} = \sqrt{4} \cdot \sqrt{\pi} = 2\sqrt{\pi}$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
